title: Establish arcmux daemon on boyan-workspace
description: Install and start the canonical arcmux service on this host so local clients can monitor its substrate API, without touching Voxtop or Tailscale.
design: Use the repository Makefile and its systemd user-service path when available; preserve any existing process/data after inspection; no Voxtop/Tailscale changes.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Canonical repo exists at /home/blin/Projects/arcmux. 2. Repo-standard build/install/start lifecycle succeeds without replacing existing state. 3. Daemon remains running under the repo-supported service lifecycle and listens on 127.0.0.1:7777. 4. HTTP /profiles returns status 200 and valid JSON; a supported health-like endpoint is checked and its exact status/body recorded. 5. Relevant repo tests/smoke checks pass or concrete blockers are documented.
notes: Host setup complete. Canonical HTTPS repo cloned to /home/blin/Projects/arcmux. Installed official Go 1.26.1 with published SHA-256 verified. make install succeeded. Installed and enabled ~/.config/systemd/user/arcmux.service because the Makefile nohup fallback cannot survive this execution harness and the repo explicitly supports Linux systemd units. Final make validate report /home/blin/Projects/arcmux/.validate-reports/2026-07-14-06-16.json passed all seven steps: gofmt, go vet, go test, make build, test bootstrap, test pulse-wake, test grpc-rt. Makefile-managed restart moved PID 3788409 to 3789208; unit active and running, enabled, NRestarts 0; listener 127.0.0.1:7777. GET /profiles returned HTTP 200 application/json with body {"profiles":[]}. GET /sessions returned HTTP 200 application/json with body {"sessions":[]}. No HTTP health route is registered: GET /health and /healthz each returned HTTP 404 text/plain with body 404 page not found. Supported arcmux info JSON reported running true, daemon_pid 3789208, session_count 0, version 0.1.0. Non-blocking risk: logs warn that OTLP trace export to localhost:4318 is refused when no collector runs.
close_reason: Established and verified the repo-standard arcmux build/install/service lifecycle on boyan-workspace; all acceptance criteria passed, with unsupported health paths documented exactly.

title: Record turn objective contract in arcmux hook state
description: When an agent is launched under arcmux, each turn should maintain a compact concrete record of the active goal, success verification, and path taken/planned so user or agent steers can operate at those three levels without bloating over time.
design: Add a compact TurnContract struct to hook session state. Extend arcmux hook with optional --goal, --verification, and --path flags that atomically replace the current contract fields. Hook scripts derive best-effort concrete values from available payload/transcript metadata and pass them only when ARCMUX_SESSION_ID is set.
acceptance_criteria: 1. arcmux hook state schema has a compact per-session turn contract with concrete goal, verification, and path fields. 2. arcmux hook CLI can update that contract atomically from hook calls without creating duplicate/bloated history. 3. Codex and generic arcmux hook scripts pass available turn metadata into arcmux hook only for arcmux-parent sessions. 4. Tests cover contract consolidation and existing hook state behavior remains compatible.
notes: User clarified this belongs in the arcmux hook path, not only agent instructions.
close_reason: Implemented compact arcmux hook turn_contract state with goal, success_verification, and path; extended arcmux hook flags and Codex/generic bridges; documented schema. Verified with go test ./internal/hooks ./cmd/arcmux, go test ./..., and a direct go run ./cmd/arcmux hook smoke that wrote turn_contract JSON.

title: Hook-authoritative idle detection for tmux sessions
description: Monitor declares working->idle from 10s screen quiescence, ignoring the turn_end hook signal already written to session state. Drive idle from hook turn_end (anchored to a per-turn WorkingSince timestamp) for fast+authoritative idle; keep quiescence as fallback for non-hook agents and as a backstop against a dropped hook. Also suppress false idle while the hook authoritatively reports working.
close_reason: Implemented hook-authoritative idle detection (turn_end anchored to WorkingSince), quiescence retained as fallback/backstop

title: Exec session/process GC: exited one-shot exec handles pile up in agent list + hung codex exec subprocesses never reaped
description:
Two related leaks in internal/daemon/exec_transport.go + daemon.go.

LEAK 1 (visible in voxtop Agents tab, IMG_6211): one-shot AutoClose exec sessions (arcmux-cli exec without --keep) set StateExited on completion (waitExecPrompt) but are NEVER removed from d.sessions. persistSessions() only excludes terminal sessions from the on-disk file (so they clear only on daemon restart); ListSessions() returns the full in-memory map unfiltered; HTTP /sessions (handleSessionsList) and gRPC ListSessions return them -> voxtop shows piles of codex_exec-* / claude_exec-* marked 'Exited' (22m, 28m, 35m, 1h old) with live call buttons. Class-agnostic (codex AND claude).

LEAK 2 (hung subprocesses): exec subprocess built with exec.Command (no ctx binding), no Setpgid/process group; runCtx/monitor cancel is a no-op for the process; no daemon-side watchdog/timeout; client disconnect/timeout (arcmux-cli exec ctx) and daemon Stop() both orphan the process instead of killing it; killExecSession's proc.Kill() reaps only the node wrapper, orphaning the rust grandchild. Result: codex exec children stuck >3min as live children of the daemon, plus orphans after restarts.

FIX: (1) hide terminal AutoClose sessions from list views + reaper goroutine deletes them from d.sessions after a short grace (preserve the Capture-by-id window arcmux-cli exec needs); (2) Setpgid on exec cmds + watchdog that kills the process group on runCtx/request-ctx cancel + group-kill in killExecSession + optional daemon-side max-runtime watchdog; wire daemon Stop() to actually kill tracked exec process groups.

title: Per-agent voice via babysit + arcmux screen recording
description: Evolve babysit into per-agent voice: arcmux gains a screen-recording capability (1s capture -> mission-control-style dedup stitching ported to Go -> append-only per-session log), single-screen babysit context (/babysit/new?name=), recording lifecycle decoupled from any voice client, and arcmux-cli voice <name>/--voice entry. voxtop babysit capture_pane reads the log file; sends keep using /session/send. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-16-voice-screen-recording-design.md
close_reason: Implemented: screenstitch port + recorder + lifecycle + HTTP shims + babysit ?name= + CLI voice. 11 commits merged to main; all tests green; final review READY TO MERGE.

title: Exec session name collision: generated names use id[2:10] (100s bucket) → idempotency dedupe misroutes
description: Found dogfooding two concurrent 'arcmux-cli exec --agent codex' dispatches with the same --owner: CreateSession with empty name generates name=fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", agent, id[2:10]) — id[2:10] is the first 8 digits of the nanosecond timestamp, identical for creates within the same ~100s window. Same (name, owner) → findNonTerminalByNameOwner idempotent_hit returned worker A's session to worker B; B then queued WaitIdle behind A and would have delivered its prompt into A's session (wrong cwd/thread). Daemon log evidence 15:32:19 session.create.idempotent_hit. Workaround used: distinct --owner per dispatch.
acceptance_criteria: Generated session names are collision-free for rapid creates (use the full id suffix like the HTTP path does, e.g. agent+id[2:]); regression test: two CreateSession calls with empty name + same owner in the same instant yield two distinct sessions (created=true twice); consider arcmux-cli exec --name passthrough. make validate green.
close_reason: Fixed: generated session names now use the full id suffix (agent + id[2:], matching the HTTP path) so rapid same-agent creates can no longer collide into the idempotency dedupe. Regression test TestCreateSessionGeneratedNamesNeverCollide proves two same-owner empty-name creates yield two distinct sessions (created=true twice). make validate pass. CLI --name passthrough deferred (workaround no longer needed).

title: Protocol state dir: ~/data/arcmux/sessions → ~/data/mux (shared mux protocol, not app dir)
description: Boyan directive: the centralized session-state contract is a PROTOCOL (something-mux), not arcmux's private storage. Move subscriber-facing state to ~/data/mux/: sessions/<id>.json (+archived) and the hook-output JSONL (also fixes the non-durable /tmp/arcmux-hooks default). arcmux-private state (profiles registry, state.bolt, pulse) stays in ~/data/arcmux/. Includes coded legacy migration sweep + docs/tickets updated across repos.
acceptance_criteria: defaults: session_state_dir=~/data/mux/sessions, hook_output_dir=~/data/mux/hook-output; daemon startup migrates legacy ~/data/arcmux/sessions/*.json when on defaults (idempotent); make validate green; live daemon writes new path; docs/state-dir.md + llm-classes + skill updated; mission-control ticket re-pointed
close_reason: Shipped d16a076: defaults moved to ~/data/mux/{sessions,hook-output}; coded idempotent startup migration (tested, verified live — daemon log shows migrated docs and all profiles on the new dir); docs/state-dir.md reframed as the mux protocol contract with ~/data/arcmux explicitly excluded as app-private; validate.md + skill updated; mission-control ticket re-pointed.

title: LLM classes: unified codex/claude/grok with interactive tmux + headless exec
description: Boyan ask: arcmux should natively support (1) starting a tmux session (under an arcmux socket) running codex/claude/grok, with prompt delivery verified via hooks writing to the centralized state dir (~/data/arcmux) that any subscriber (arcmux, mission-control) can read; (2) headless one-shot executions (codex exec, claude -p, grok -p) — both modes managed by one LLM class per agent so adding/configuring an LLM is one place. Callers: arcmux-cli (human) or the arcmux skill in ~/agents/blin-agents/skills.
design: Class registry in internal/profile: type Class{Name, Interactive Profile, Exec Profile}; DefaultClasses()=[codex,claude,grok]; DefaultProfiles() derived (<name> + <name>_exec) so existing profile names stay stable. Grok interactive: 'grok --no-alt-screen --permission-mode bypassPermissions', ready='│ ❯', trust gate 'Run Grok Build in a project directory'→Enter, working='Ctrl+Enter:interject', HookType=grok_hooks (grok reads drop-in ~/.grok/hooks/*.json — always trusted, registration IS the file, unlike claude/codex manual registration). Grok exec: grok -p <prompt> --output-format streaming-json (verified live: thought/text chunks + end event w/ sessionId for --resume). Generic hook script extended to parse grok payload (hookEventName/toolName camelCase keys, snake_case values) + --agent ${ARCMUX_HOOK_AGENT}. Daemon: replace agent-name conditionals (create/restore/agentStartCommand/Installer.Install) with profile.HookBacked()/HookType. New ListAgents gRPC + arcmux-cli agents + arcmux-cli exec (create exec session AutoClose + SendPrompt WaitIdle + Capture). Docs: docs/state-dir.md for external subscribers (mission-control) of ~/data/arcmux. Skill updated.
acceptance_criteria: 1) internal/llm (or equivalent) defines one LLM class per agent (codex, claude, grok) carrying both its interactive tmux profile and its exec driver; profile.DefaultProfiles derives from the class registry so adding an LLM is one struct. 2) grok works in both modes: 'arcmux-cli create --agent grok' spawns it in a tmux pane under the arcmux socket; 'arcmux-cli exec --agent grok -- <prompt>' (or grok_exec profile) runs grok -p headless and returns output. 3) Hook installation is registry-driven (no hard-coded agent name conditionals in daemon create path); hook events for any LLM land in the centralized ~/data/arcmux state (sessions/<id>.json + hook-output JSONL) readable by any subscriber; documented schema/path doc exists for external consumers (mission-control). 4) Delivery verification uses hooks judge for LLMs with hooks; grok (no hooks) degrades gracefully to heuristic. 5) Discovery: callers can list available LLM classes + transports (CLI command or HTTP). 6) make validate green (gofmt, vet, go test ./..., build); unit tests cover the class registry, grok profile, exec driver routing. 7) arcmux skill (~/agents/blin-agents/skills/arcmux) updated to document both call paths.
notes: Follow-up 0a6961b: default delivery judge is now 'auto' (hooks → typesafe → heuristic cascade) per Boyan directive — hooks always win when available; previously all real daemons defaulted to typesafe. Live-verified: default-config daemon + grok delivery → judge_source=hooks.
close_reason: Shipped: internal/profile.Class registry (codex/claude/grok × interactive tmux + headless exec) with DefaultProfiles derived from classes; grok interactive profile (ready/trust/working patterns verified live) + grok_exec driver (streaming-json parser w/ resume via sessionId); hook install + env seeding + loader wrapping now keyed by profile.HookType/HookBacked() — no agent-name conditionals; grok hooks fully auto-registered via drop-in ~/.grok/hooks/arcmux-session.json; generic hook script parses both claude and grok payload dialects and attributes via ARCMUX_HOOK_AGENT; per-session grok leader socket (session_start_args) fixes hooks running in env-less shared leader (verified: leader exits with session); ListAgents gRPC + arcmux-cli agents + arcmux-cli exec (class resolution, --keep/--session resume, --json, --timeout); docs/llm-classes.md + docs/state-dir.md subscriber contract; skill updated. Verified: make validate green; unit tests for classes/parsers/installers/script dialects; live e2e scripts/e2e/hooks-judge-live.sh grok PASS (prompt_submit+turn_end in ~/data state, prompt_ingested gated); live exec smoke returned grok output. Residual: judge_source may report heuristic when screen evidence wins the first-poll race (hooks evidence still recorded); claude/codex hook registration remains manual (arcmux-kxv/arcmux-urc).

title: Real-agent e2e: hooks judge gates a live claude/codex delivery
description: Register claude arcmux-session-hook.sh in ~/.claude/settings.json (UserPromptSubmit+Stop+PreToolUse+PostToolUse); build committed repeatable e2e (scripts/e2e/hooks-judge-live.sh + make target) that starts daemon judge=hooks, spawns real claude via arcmux-cli, sends prompt, asserts prompt_ingested judge_source=hooks + session state prompt_submit. Exposed gap: daemon drops the hook script but never registers it in claude settings, so native hook never fires.
close_reason: Claude arcmux-session-hook.sh now registered in ~/.claude/settings.json (blueprint template claude/settings.json + live; blin-agents 2edaf9d) on UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop with test -f guard. Repeatable e2e existed (scripts/e2e/hooks-judge-live.sh / make validate-e2e-hooks); ran live post-registration: PASS with prompt_ingested judge_source=hooks confidence=0.97 and session-state prompt_submit evidence. In-daemon auto-registration remains tracked as arcmux-urc.

title: Split delivery judges + add hooks-based judge
description: Move TypesafeJudge into judge_typesafe.go, add HooksJudge (judge_hooks.go) reading ~/data/arcmux/sessions/<id>.json mutated by an 'arcmux hook' CLI from claude+codex hooks. Config [delivery].judge picks one judge. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-judge-split-and-hooks-judge-design.md
notes: Path update (d16a076): hooks judge session state now reads the PROTOCOL dir ~/data/mux/sessions (was ~/data/arcmux/sessions); default judge is now 'auto' (hooks→typesafe→heuristic, 0a6961b).
close_reason: Epic complete: judges split (judge_typesafe.go/judge_hooks.go, 77d70a7), hooks judge reads protocol state ~/data/mux/sessions (d16a076), config [delivery].judge selects, default now auto-cascade hooks→typesafe→heuristic (0a6961b), live e2e green for claude+grok (judge_source=hooks 0.97).

title: Session state never transitions working->idle (tmux transport): stuck 'working' forever after agent finishes
description: After a prompt is delivered SetState(working) is called, but nothing ever transitions a tmux-backed session back to idle: the health monitor only does idle->working, working->stuck, and ->exited; the hook watcher tails Stop events into a map that no consumer reads. Observed: codex and claude both report state=working 70s+ after answering and going visually idle at their prompt. Fix: add a screen-quiescence working->idle transition to the health monitor (output unchanged for a quiescence window AND no WorkingIndicator present).
close_reason: Fixed: added screen-quiescence working->idle transition to health monitor (quiescentIdle + idleQuiescence, per-session runState). Guarded by WorkingIndicator so a busy-but-quiet agent is not falsely idled; added WorkingIndicator 'esc to interrupt' to claude profile (codex already had 'Working'). Verified live on both agents incl. a 25s silent task that correctly stayed 'working'. Unit tests added in health/monitor_test.go.

title: Claude handshake marks session 'failed': ready pattern '>' never matches v2.x prompt glyph '❯'
description: The claude profile ReadyPattern is '>' but Claude Code v2.x renders its prompt as '❯' (U+276F) and shows no bare '>' on screen, so waitForReadyPattern times out after 25s and performHandshake fails -> session state set to 'failed'. The pane is actually fully ready (Remote Control active) and SendPrompt works, so the 'failed' state is a false negative that misleads status/ready callers. Fix: use a ReadyPattern that matches a genuinely-ready Claude TUI (e.g. the persistent footer 'bypass permissions on'), not the pre-launch shell prompt glyph.
close_reason: Fixed: claude profile ReadyPattern '>' -> 'Remote Control active' (matches the --remote-control footer present in both default and HTTP launch paths; '>' never matched v2.x glyph '❯'). Verified live: handshake now reaches idle, not failed. Regression test added in profile_test.go.

# Replace per-session Claude hook scripts with one session-aware hook

## Problem
Boyan directive from Discord 1509663488170131686: arcmux currently writes ~/.claude/hooks/arcmux-s-<session>.sh per Claude session. Change it so arcmux installs/uses a single generic hook script that derives the session-specific output path from environment supplied to the Claude session (for example ARCMUX_SESSION_ID and ARCMUX_HOOK_OUTPUT_DIR). Remove/clean up existing generated session-specific hooks like arcmux-s-*.sh. Preserve arcmux's watcher/status/ready behavior.

Follow-up design directive from Discord 1509665898401890495: if a shared handoff point is needed, use a /tmp/arcmux directory where the tmux session creator writes the session-specific environment, and make the tmux loader read that environment before launching claude/codex/etc. This keeps the hook generic while making session identity/runtime paths available to the launched agent process.

## Acceptance
No new per-session Claude hook script files are created; one generic arcmux hook is installed/updated idempotently; spawned Claude sessions receive the env needed for that hook to write the correct per-session JSONL; if file-backed env handoff is needed, the session creator writes a per-session env file under /tmp/arcmux and the tmux loader loads it before launching the agent; /tmp/arcmux and env files are permissioned safely and parsed/escaped so an untrusted writable file cannot inject shell; existing arcmux-s-*.sh files are removed/ignored by migration/cleanup; tests cover install idempotency, env-derived output path, tmux-loader env loading, cleanup, and watcher/status compatibility; PR/merge documents operational cleanup.

Post-merge deployment acceptance from Discord 1509668087086059530: after the refactor merges, rebuild/restart the live `test-elonco` elonco/arcmux runtime so this profile is actually running the new generic-hook + /tmp/arcmux env-handoff behavior. Verify on the live restarted profile that no new per-session hook scripts are created, the generic hook/env path is active, and existing stale `arcmux-s-*.sh` cleanup has run or is demonstrably ignored/removed.

## Duplicate Search
Query: Replace per-session Claude hook scripts with one session-aware hook Boyan directive from Discord 1509663488170131686: arcmux currently writes ~/.claude/hooks/arcmux-s-<session>.sh per Claude session. Change it so arcmux installs/uses a single generic hook script that derives the session-specific output path from environment supplied to the Claude session (for example ARCMUX_SESSION_ID and ARCMUX_HOOK_OUTPUT_DIR). Remove/clean up existing generated session-specific hooks like arcmux-s-*.sh. Preserve arcmux's watcher/status/ready behavior. No new per-session Claude hook script files are created; one generic arcmux hook is installed/updated idempotently; spawned Claude sessions receive the env needed for that hook to write the correct per-session JSONL; existing arcmux-s-*.sh files are removed/ignored by migration/cleanup; tests cover install idempotency, env-derived output path, cleanup, and watcher/status compatibility; PR/merge documents operational cleanup.
Similar tickets: none

## Dependencies
Blocked by: none
Blocks: none

## Events
- 2026-05-28T14:39:52-07:00 — pr-merge — manager:platform: Merged PR #1 to arcmux main via squash (9e28ca0) after 4-dim review. Security gate verified in code: envfile.go does Lstat symlink-reject + uid-ownership + 0o077 perm mask + ARCMUX_ allowlist + NUL/newline reject + POSIX single-quote escaping; loader (daemon.go agentStartCommand:582) prefixes eval $(<abs-arcmux> hook-env <id>) via os.Executable, evals arcmux's OWN quoted output, never sources raw file; hookenv.go fails safe (error->no stdout, exit 0). go test ./... all green, gofmt+vet clean. MaliciousValueRoundTripsAsLiteral + perms/symlink/disallowed-key + fail-safe tests present. Post-merge live deploy/verification PENDING (manager owns detached rebuild+restart).
- 2026-05-28T14:05:41-07:00 — create — elon:test-elonco: priority 1
- 2026-05-28T14:13:00-07:00 — refine — elon:test-elonco: Boyan added preferred design: session creator writes env under /tmp/arcmux; tmux loader reads session env before launching claude/codex; keep generic hook and add permissions/safe parsing guardrails.
- 2026-05-28T14:22:00-07:00 — deploy-requirement — elon:test-elonco: Boyan requested rebuild/restart after the refactor so live `test-elonco` runs the new behavior; added post-merge deployment acceptance and live verification.
- 2026-05-28T14:55:00-07:00 — pr-open — ic:platform:1: opened PR #1 https://github.com/lin-labs/arcmux/pull/1 (branch arcmux-hooks-1-generic-session-hook; one generic hook + /tmp/arcmux safe handoff + coded legacy sweep; go test ./... green; design-first + post-merge deploy section; no self-merge). Note: contract c-3b9e539f3e02 supersedes c-703ae72bbf52.
close_reason: PR #1 merged (9e28ca0); generic session-aware hook + /tmp/arcmux env handoff shipped and since extended (grok dialect, ARCMUX_HOOK_AGENT). Post-merge live verification satisfied by repeated live runs 2026-06-09: daemon restarts on new binary, generic hook fires via env handoff (judge_source=hooks e2e), no per-session scripts regenerated, legacy sweep coded in CleanupLegacyScripts.

title: Use antenna marker for remote PTY tabs
description: Update the cmux remote PTY surface-tab renamer to use a compact antenna marker instead of the [remote] text prefix.
design: Use 📡 as the sole visible remote marker. Treat existing [remote] as a legacy remote marker, strip leading emoji/bracket marker chains, and normalize remote titles to 📡 <body>.
acceptance_criteria: Given a remote SSH/mosh surface title currently starting with [remote], the live title becomes 📡 <body>. Given remote SSH/mosh titles with leading [ssh]/[mosh]/host prefixes, they normalize to 📡 <body>. Given local tabs, titles remain unchanged. Rerunning dry-run after apply reports no changes.
close_reason: Updated remote PTY tab renamer to use 📡 as the sole marker. Verified live remote titles are 📡 ax/tmux..., local titles unchanged, dry-run empty, plist lint and py_compile pass, LaunchAgent last exit 0.

title: Normalize remote PTY title prefix
description: Update the cmux remote PTY tab renamer so SSH/mosh surface titles use a single [remote] marker instead of stacking [remote] with [mosh]/host prefixes.
design: Normalize remote surface titles to exactly one leading [remote] marker. Detect remote titles by [mosh]/[ssh] or ssh/mosh command markers, strip any leading bracket-prefix chain, then rename to [remote] <body>.
acceptance_criteria: Given a remote SSH/mosh title such as [remote] [mosh] [blin-labs] tmux..., the live surface title becomes [remote] tmux... . Given local tabs, their titles remain unchanged. Rerunning dry-run after apply reports no changes.
close_reason: Updated remote PTY tab renamer to normalize to a single [remote] prefix by stripping leading bracket-prefix chains from SSH/mosh titles. Verified live titles are [remote] ax/tmux..., local tabs unchanged, dry-run empty, plist lint and py_compile pass.

title: Prefix remote PTY surface tabs
description: Since cmux does not expose surface-tab colors, distinguish SSH/mosh remote PTY tabs by renaming remote surface tabs with a [remote] prefix when they lack an existing remote/lab prefix.
design: Use cmux tab-action rename with explicit --workspace and --surface. Detect remote PTY tabs from titles containing [mosh]/[ssh] or starting with mosh/ssh commands, and add [remote] only when the first bracket prefix is not already remote/lab-like.
acceptance_criteria: Given a terminal surface title that indicates SSH or mosh and does not already start with [remote] or a lab-like prefix, the tab is renamed with a leading [remote]. Given local terminal surfaces, their titles are unchanged. Given already-prefixed remote tabs, rerunning the automation is idempotent. Verified with dry-run output and live cmux tree titles.
close_reason: Implemented cmux surface-tab rename automation. Remote SSH/mosh terminal surfaces without a remote/lab-like first prefix are renamed with [remote], local tabs are unchanged, reruns are idempotent. Verified dry-run changes, live cmux tree titles, plist lint, py_compile, and LaunchAgent runs=2 last exit 0.

title: Color remote PTY tabs
description: Distinguish local PTY tabs from SSH/mosh-backed remote PTY tabs so local stays plain and remote shows a light red tab.
design: Use local cmux automation because installed cmux exposes workspace tab color via workspace-action but not per-surface tab color via tab-action. Classify remote PTYs from live cmux tree terminal surface titles prefixed [mosh] or [ssh], set workspace tab custom color to light red, and clear only colors previously managed by the automation when a workspace is no longer remote.
acceptance_criteria: Local PTY tabs keep the existing plain styling. SSH or mosh remote PTY tabs render with a light red tab indicator/background. The implementation is verified with targeted tests or the closest project validation command.
notes: Correction after user clarification: desired target is surface/horizontal tab color, not workspace color. Current cmux 0.64.13 exposes workspace-action set-color only; tab.action/surface.action supported actions do not include color, and live surface.action set_color returns invalid_params Unknown tab action. Local workspace-color LaunchAgent was unloaded and colors cleared.
close_reason: Implemented local cmux automation that detects [mosh]/[ssh] remote PTY terminal surfaces, colors their workspace tabs #FFB3B3, leaves local tabs plain, and runs via LaunchAgent every 5 seconds. Verified py_compile, plist lint, LaunchAgent last exit 0, and live cmux workspace colors.

title: Expose per-session recording (babysit-enabled) flag in GET /sessions
description: sessionSummary now carries recording bool (json: recording) populated from RecordingStatus(snap.ID), so the Vox app can filter the agent list to babysit-capable/callable sessions. Server-side done in internal/daemon/http.go. Pairs with voxtop Agents-tab babysit filter toggle.

title: Mute 'command finished' notifications (zero-signal noise)
description: Moved from mission-control-haw (misfiled there — mc emits no OS notifications; arcmux does). All notifications that merely say a command/tool finished are pure noise (0 signal). Suppress that class entirely while keeping meaningful ones (agent needs input, error/failure, blocked, done-with-result). Emitter lives in internal/manager/cmuxcli/notify.go; hook events (Notification/Stop) flow through cmd/arcmux. Audit the hook->notify path and drop the 'completed'/'command finished' class.
acceptance_criteria: Given an agent/tool merely finishes a command, no OS notification fires. Given a meaningful event (needs-input, error, blocked, done-with-result), a notification still fires. Verified live by triggering both a plain command-finish and a needs-input event and observing only the latter notifies.

title: SendRequest dedup_key wire field + server-side dedup
description:
Split from `arcmux-send-dedup-proto-1` to isolate the arcmux-side prerequisite in the repo that actually owns it. This bead owns only the substrate half in `~/Projects/arcmux`: add a `dedup_key` field to `SendRequest`, teach the daemon `Send` path to deduplicate repeated sends within a bounded per-session cache/window, preserve legacy behavior when the key is omitted, and land the Go test coverage that proves the semantics. The follow-on elonco bead will depend on the shipped capability from this bead before regenerating vendored stubs and forwarding `dedup_key` from Python.

Deliverable: one PR against `lin-labs/arcmux` touching the proto, generated Go stubs if tracked, daemon/server send handling, and Go tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `proto/arcmux/v1/arcmux.proto` adds a `dedup_key` field to `SendRequest` without breaking callers that omit it.
- [ ] Server-side send handling deduplicates duplicate sends within a bounded per-session cache/window and returns the original `msg_id`/result on a hit.
- [ ] Dedup does not cross sessions; missing `dedup_key` preserves current behavior; bounded/LRU or expiry behavior is covered by Go tests.
- [ ] The PR includes the repo-owned generated Go stub updates required by the proto change.
- [ ] Verification is captured with targeted Go tests covering duplicate send from fresh callers, different sessions not deduping, missing-key behavior, and cache bound/expiry behavior.
notes:
Cross-repo review path for this bead: the IC opens the PR on `lin-labs/arcmux`, then writes state first with `bd update <id> --set-metadata review=requested --set-metadata pr=<url>` in the arcmux repo, and wakes the EM with `elonco queue add lab-elonco --team platform --kind review --body "[REVIEW] <id> \"<title>\" PR <url>"`. Manager review is against `gh pr diff -R lin-labs/arcmux` plus the bead acceptance criteria; verdict lives in bead metadata `review=approved` or `review=changes`. The IC merges its own arcmux PR after approval. Ship capability with `bd ship send-dedup-key-wire` once the bead is closed so downstream external deps unblock.
Manager check 2026-06-11 PT: accept the repo-scoped split already in place. arcmux-39r owns the arcmux proto/daemon/Go-test prerequisite and exports capability send-dedup-key-wire; elonco-e6i remains blocked in /home/blin/Projects/elonco until that capability ships. Cleared stale manager:platform assignee so the ready arcmux bead is pullable by an IC under the pull model.

title: Manage Codex remote-server singleton
description: arcmux should be able to start Codex remote-server by running cdx remote-server in a tmux session, stop it by killing that process, and refuse to start a duplicate when the per-computer singleton is already established.
design: HTTP /session/new keeps Claude on cld --remote-control, changes Codex to the requested interactive tmux command cdx remote-server, and guards Codex as a singleton by returning an existing active Codex remote-server session instead of creating another tmux pane.
acceptance_criteria:
- arcmux can start Codex remote-server in tmux using cdx remote-server.
- Stop kills the Codex remote-server process/session.
- Start is idempotent: if a Codex remote-server is already established/running, arcmux reports that state instead of starting another.
- Status exposes enough state for the Vox app to grey out Codex when already running.
- Go tests/build pass.
notes: cdx is not on non-interactive PATH in this shell, but tmux launch sends the command into an interactive shell where the user's cdx wrapper/alias is expected to exist.
close_reason: Implemented HTTP Codex remote-server launch via cdx remote-server, tagged remote_server/current_command state in create/list responses, guarded Codex remote-server as a live singleton, and close now kills the tmux session when available. Verified go test ./internal/daemon -run 'TestAgentStartCommand|TestIsCodexRemoteServerSnapshot' and git diff --check; go test ./... only fails unrelated internal/config TestDeliveryDefaults path expectation.

title: Optionally auto-register claude/codex hooks in agent config on daemon start
description: Today arcmux drops arcmux-session-hook.sh / arcmux-codex-hook.sh but does NOT register them in ~/.claude/settings.json or ~/.codex/hooks.json — registration is machine-local manual. Consider idempotent RMW auto-registration (preserving existing entries) so the hooks judge works on a fresh machine without manual steps. Surfaced by the real-agent e2e (scripts/e2e/hooks-judge-live.sh).
acceptance_criteria: Config [hooks] auto_register (default false, opt-in per issue title). When true, daemon Start idempotently RMW-registers: (1) the generic session hook into ~/.claude/settings.json hooks (UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop, test -f guarded command, preserving ALL existing entries + unrelated keys byte-for-byte where untouched; skip events already containing the arcmux command); (2) the codex bridge into ~/.codex/hooks.json (same RMW discipline, matching the existing live shape). Never duplicates entries on repeated starts; malformed/missing files handled (create minimal valid file only if absent); unit tests cover fresh file, existing-other-hooks preservation, idempotent re-run, and malformed-JSON refusal (non-fatal warn). make validate green.
notes: Update 2026-06-09: claude hook is now registered at the BLUEPRINT layer (blin-agents claude/settings.json, commit 2edaf9d) — live e2e passes judge_source=hooks. This issue remains open only for the in-daemon auto-registration variant (useful on machines not managed by the blueprint). State dir is now ~/data/mux/sessions (d16a076).
close_reason: Added opt-in [hooks].auto_register (default false) and daemon-start RMW registration for Claude settings.json and Codex hooks.json; tests cover fresh files, existing-hook/key preservation, idempotent reruns, malformed JSON refusal, /Users/blin duplicate detection, and daemon opt-in wiring; make validate overall: pass.

title: Run babysit session end-to-end on voxtop repo (client-visible)
description: Exercise the babysit subsystem live: register voxtop in arcmux projects.toml (repo_cwd + plan_globs), restart daemon, spawn a Claude worker in the voxtop repo, mint a project-scoped call context (server=blin-labs tailscale DNS), and deliver a vox://babysit deep link so the iOS Vox client can see the invite in its tray and join the voice call.
notes:
Live: voxtop registered in ~/.config/arcmux/projects.toml (repo_cwd ~/Projects/voxtop, plan_globs docs/*.md + docs/plans/*.md → 17 plan refs). arcmux --user restarted. Worker 'vox-worker' (claude, idle) spawned in voxtop repo. Context ctx-8792ca9c5bee minted (server=blin-labs.tail82611a.ts.net:5060, ttl 2h, expires 21:56 PT), resolves via /babysit/context with 1 pane. Deep link + QR delivered for the phone. Pending: Boyan taps link → invite appears in Vox tray → joins call.
Verified headless: live WS smoke (scripts/smoke-babysit-ws.py in voxtop) PASSes against running server+arcmux — valid context accepted (session.created), bogus rejected (session.error). Added hermetic regression guard (HandoffGateTests, 12/12 green) + refactored the connect gate into babysit_handoff_rejected(). iOS UI tap-flow leg (Mac-only) delegated to voxtop-nq8. voxtop changes pushed to origin/main.

title: B2: project registry + /sessions?project= filter
description: Closes Codex C1/A5/M1: arcmux is pure substrate with no project field today.
acceptance_criteria: projects.toml (slug->repo_cwd,plan_globs) loaded by daemon; /sessions?project=X returns sessions whose cwd is under repo_cwd or owner_id==project:X; unknown project -> empty list, not error.
close_reason: Added internal/project registry (projects.toml, slug->repo_cwd+plan_globs) + /sessions?project= filter (cwd-under-repo OR owner_id tag); nil-safe daemon load; unknown project -> empty. 7 tests pass; docs updated.

title: B3: /babysit/new + /babysit/context (bbolt context store)
description: Closes Codex M2. Distinct ?context= token, NOT ?token= (that's the API key).
acceptance_criteria: /babysit/new?project= mints a context (panes+repo_cwd+plan paths+server), persists to daemon bbolt with TTL, returns connect handle wss://host/v1/realtime/converse?context=<tok>; /babysit/context?context=<tok> resolves it; expired/unknown -> 404.
close_reason: Added /babysit/new (mint project-scoped context: panes+repo_cwd+plan_refs+server, bbolt-persisted, TTL) and /babysit/context (resolve, lazy expiry). Distinct ?context= token. store bucket+methods + 5 daemon tests pass; docs updated.

title: B4: server-side bearer auth on arcmux HTTP
description: Closes Codex C4: :7777 currently unauthenticated; authorize() is a client no-op.
acceptance_criteria: Optional shared-secret bearer required for mutating/remote requests; loopback stays open; config-driven; 401 on bad/missing when enabled.
close_reason: Added optional config http_auth_token + withAuth middleware: loopback always open, non-loopback requires Bearer (constant-time compare), 401 otherwise; disabled when empty. 2 tests (table + 401) pass; docs Authentication section added.

title: B1: HTTP /session/capture + /session/send shims
description: Grounded starting point: gRPC Capture/Send already exist; expose over HTTP. Updates docs/http-api.md roadmap section.
acceptance_criteria: POST/GET /session/capture?name= returns pane contents via gRPC Capture; /session/send?name=&text= delivers via gRPC Send; errors mirror existing handlers; http_test.go coverage.
close_reason: Implemented /session/capture + /session/send HTTP shims over daemon Capture/SendPrompt; 6 handler tests pass; docs/http-api.md updated.

title: C: iOS multi-server support

acceptance_criteria: Vox stores a saved server list + active selection; picker in Settings; migrates the existing single serverHost; notification 'server' is honored.
close_reason: Multi-server implemented: ServerEntry model + savedServers in VoxDefaults, AppState add/remove/select + migration seed, SettingsView picker+add. serverHost stays the active-host accessor. CODE COMPLETE; needs macOS xcodegen+build to verify (no Swift toolchain on Linux).

close_reason: iOS push+tray+deep-link+connect code complete: BabysitInvite/Store, BabysitAppDelegate (APNs reg+token+routing), vox://babysit handling, ConverseService ?context= + babysit mode, ConverseView/MainTabView routing, BabysitTrayView, Settings entry, Info.plist/entitlements. Plists validated. Needs macOS build + APNs key/provisioning + arcmux push sender (operational).

close_reason: Web prototype built in ~/Projects/voice-controlled-pm (index.html/app.js/serve.sh): mic->WS->relay, audio playback, tool/transcript feed. JS syntax-checked, serves 200. Full voice loop validation needs live voxtop server+xAI key+mic (manual).

title: A: voxtop babysit relay mode + tools

acceptance_criteria: MODES['babysit'] + FUNCTION_SCHEMAS/TOOLS for toolset; main.py validates ?context= and forces babysit mode+scope; run_relay accepts context; per-context repo_cwd (not global VOICE_TOOLS_CWD); two-phase mechanical confirm gate on writes; bead_* tools with structured args.
close_reason: voxtop babysit mode shipped: MODES[babysit] + babysit_tools (pane/plan/beads tools), two-phase confirm gate, ?context= handoff in main.py. 9 tests + 25 relay regression tests pass.

title: B: arcmux babysit subsystem (keystone)
description: Mint/lookup call context, add capture+send, project->panes filter, push trigger hook. Everything hangs off this.
acceptance_criteria: Project registry (slug->repo_cwd+plan_globs); /sessions?project= filter; /session/capture + /session/send HTTP shims over existing gRPC Capture/Send; /babysit/new + /babysit/context backed by daemon bbolt with TTL; server-side bearer auth on HTTP (loopback open).
close_reason: B keystone complete: B1 capture/send shims, B2 project registry+filter, B3 babysit context mint/lookup (bbolt+TTL), B4 server-side bearer auth. Full arcmux suite green; docs/http-api.md updated. A (voxtop relay) is now unblocked.

title: Babysitter voice mode: project-scoped voice control plane over arcmux
description: Voice call, summoned to the phone, that comes up scoped to one project: its arcmux panes, plan/PRD, and Beads. Read/redirect/dispatch agents, edit plan in place, CRUD beads — all by voice with confirm-readback. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-babysitter-voice-mode-design.md
close_reason: Babysitter voice mode shipped end-to-end: B (arcmux subsystem, go tests green), A (voxtop relay mode + two-phase confirm gate, 84 server tests green), A.5 (web prototype built). C (iOS multi-server) and D (iOS push/tray/connect) code-complete pending macOS Xcode build + APNs provisioning + arcmux push sender.

title: arcmux pane registry shows stale panes after a profile reload
description:
Captured via feedback-dev from the LabOps cockpit for service `arcmux`.

User feedback:
- arcmux pane registry shows stale panes after a profile reload
- bug; success is registry reflecting live panes within 1s of reload
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The reported behavior for `arcmux` is addressed
- [ ] Change is verified with `make validate` and a live check

title: Browser E2E: make restart converges to one daemon
description:
## Summary
Browser E2E coverage has observed occasional double-spawn behavior after restart. The scoped restart path is `make restart`: after it completes and settles, process state should converge to exactly one daemon instance.

## Expected behavior
Running `make restart` repeatedly leaves one daemon running, with no duplicate process surviving from the previous run or appearing during restart convergence.

## Scope
This is specifically about `make restart`, not browser reloads, test runner retries, manual daemon restarts, or service container restarts.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Repeated `make restart` runs settle with exactly one daemon instance running
- [ ] No duplicate daemon process remains after restart convergence
- [ ] Browser E2E coverage asserts the `make restart` restart path
- [ ] Failure output reports the observed daemon instance count

title: make restart leaves exactly one daemon
description:
When `make restart` is used, the restart path can leave a duplicate daemon behind. Only one process binds the listening port, but an extra daemon remains running, making process state ambiguous after restart.

Expected behavior: `make restart` is idempotent and collapses the process set to exactly one daemon.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Running `make restart` while a daemon is already running leaves exactly one daemon process.
- [ ] Only the surviving daemon owns the listening port.
- [ ] No duplicate or stale daemon process remains after restart completes.
- [ ] Repeating `make restart` multiple times preserves the one-daemon invariant.

title: Tombstone legacy embedded elonco role prompts
description: Follow-up from elonco-wde PR #68: elonco now owns reviewed role prompts in src/elonco/prompts/roles and mirrors them to the vault. Arcmux still documents or may carry legacy embedded role prompt seed paths (internal/manager/roles/files, roles/seeds.go) from the old org-aware manager mode. Track the arcmux-side cleanup so no third copy silently drifts outside the repo↔vault guard.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Confirm whether arcmux still ships embedded role prompt files or seed code in internal/manager/roles/files, roles/seeds.go, or successor paths.
- [ ] Delete, tombstone, or clearly mark any remaining embedded elonco role prompt copies as deprecated/non-authoritative.
- [ ] Update arcmux docs/tests so role prompt ownership points to elonco repo/vault mirror, not arcmux embedded copies.
- [ ] The change stays arcmux-side and does not reintroduce org-aware role-prompt mutation into arcmux.
notes: Discovered during manager review of elonco-wde PR #68. PR #68 made elonco repo role files the reviewed source of truth and vault the mirror; this bead tracks the arcmux-side third-copy cleanup/tombstone so stale embedded role prompt references do not drift.

title: Rename Codex remote command to remote-control
description: The HTTP session launcher and its tests still identify the Codex remote command as 'cdx remote-server'. The intended command is 'cdx remote-control', so user-facing session launch behavior and detection logic should use that name consistently.
design: Update the daemon command constant and the daemon HTTP tests together so command emission and singleton detection stay aligned.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Starting a Codex session through the HTTP daemon uses `cdx remote-control`
- [ ] Existing Codex remote-session detection treats `cdx remote-control` as the singleton command
- [ ] Focused daemon tests pass with the updated command name
close_reason: Updated the HTTP daemon Codex remote command constant and matching singleton-detection tests from 'cdx remote-server' to 'cdx remote-control'; verified with go test ./internal/daemon.

title: Define remote session, artifact, and surface-binding protocol
description: Once the mesh is reliable, expose typed arcmux queries/events for remote agent sessions, goals, histories, documents, PRs, and other artifacts. Define a stable locator that lets Mission Control map a cmux surface to the exact remote arcmux peer, profile, and session without guessing from titles or screen text.
design: Layer typed request/response and event streams over the proven mesh. Use arcmux session identity as authority; cmux/Mission Control stores an explicit RemoteSessionLocator on each connecting surface. Keep artifact transport generic and consumers provider-specific.
acceptance_criteria: 1. A versioned remote-session locator identifies device/peer, arcmux profile, session ID, and optional transport binding. 2. Arcmux can query session summary/detail and subscribe to typed updates across the mesh. 3. Artifact envelopes cover goals, session histories, docs, branches/commits, and PR references with provenance and freshness. 4. Surface bindings are explicit, durable enough for Mission Control restart, and become stale rather than silently retargeting. 5. Authorization and redaction prevent raw secrets/tool output from crossing by default. 6. Contract tests prove local and remote responses are semantically identical and tolerate version skew.
notes:
Phase 2 contract: keep mesh protocol/subprotocol v1 and negotiate additive capabilities. Stable session identity is authenticated device + required profile scope + session ID; transport attachment is non-identity metadata. Public DTOs are allowlists and exclude env, credentials, backend IDs, tmux targets, commands/prompts, raw screen/tool output, and unrestricted paths. Initial methods are read-only session list/get and artifact list/get plus typed subscriptions. Surface bindings key cmux stable surface UUID to an exact locator; disconnect marks stale, reconnect syncs, and a complete snapshot alone may mark gone. Local/remote views share one root-plus-profile catalog. See vault plan resilient-device-mesh.md.

Released via arcmux PR 4, merged as 2efe664, to ref launchd and devbox systemd. Live validation: both peers negotiated rpc/events/sessions/artifacts capabilities; devbox listed all 16 ref sessions fresh with safe DTOs; PR 4 mirrored as a structured pull-request artifact with title/URL redacted on wire; a temporary cmux surface binding resolved to the exact ref session and was removed; stopping devbox marked ref disconnected, restart reconnected automatically with desired topics restored and both topic inventories fresh; all 16 ref agent panes survived both releases and the outage. State roots are 0700 and JSON records/registries 0600. Final make validate and focused race gates pass.
close_reason: Remote session, artifact, subscription, and surface-binding protocol released and verified live across ref and devbox.

title: Build resilient Tailscale WebSocket peer mesh
description: Give each arcmux daemon a durable authenticated peer connection suitable for a laptop that repeatedly leaves and rejoins Wi-Fi or Tailscale. Remote arcmux and its agents must continue independently during arbitrarily long disconnects; clients see explicit stale/unavailable state and recover automatically without restarting agents.
design: Implement the mesh as a generic arcmux transport with a dedicated endpoint and status API. Prefer outbound laptop-to-remote connections for the first two-device path; persist peer configuration and only ephemeral health metadata. Keep ref's arcmux control API loopback-only. Surface disconnection as expected stale visibility, not as an agent/session failure.
acceptance_criteria: 1. A one-time peer/serve configuration creates an authenticated WebSocket path without exposing the existing loopback control API. 2. The connector uses bounded exponential backoff with jitter, reacts promptly to network restoration, has heartbeat/dead-peer detection, and never busy-loops. 3. Long disconnects do not affect local/remote agent sessions; peer status exposes connected, disconnected/stale, last success, last error, and next retry. 4. Reconnection restores queries without daemon or agent restart. 5. Protocol negotiation, duplicate connection handling, graceful shutdown, malformed frames, auth failure, and backpressure are tested. 6. Unit/integration tests, gofmt, vet, full Go tests, local daemon smoke, and a live ref-to-remote reconnect smoke pass. 7. Operational docs and service lifecycle remain idempotent.
notes:
Lifecycle refinement: pairing must hot-reload only the mesh subsystem through the loopback admin API. `arcmux mesh serve` / `join` must take effect against an already-running daemon without restarting arcmux or its managed agent panes; if the daemon is offline, persist config for next start and say so explicitly.
Implemented and validated on ref and devbox: loopback-only protocol-v1 mesh, hashed per-peer credentials, hot reload without session restart, infinite jittered reconnect, heartbeat/stale/dead status, CLI status/ping/serve/join, and operational docs. Full make validate, full Go suite, focused race tests, and real cross-device Tailscale loss/recovery passed; observed application RTT 69-71 ms. Production merge, release, and persistent pairing follow immediately in this turn.
close_reason: Phase 1 implementation and acceptance validation complete; cross-device disconnect/reconnect recovered without restarting the surviving daemon or any agent sessions.

title: Federate arcmux sessions and artifacts across devices
description: Build the staged arcmux foundation for resilient device connectivity, remote session visibility, artifact exchange, notifications, surface-to-session identity, and eventually safe cross-device agent handoff. The laptop may disappear from Wi-Fi or Tailscale for long periods; remote agents must remain healthy and become queryable again after reconnection.
design: Arcmux owns connectivity and substrate protocols. Phase 1 is a resilient authenticated peer mesh. Phase 2 adds typed session/artifact queries and stable surface bindings. Phase 3 adds explicit cross-device handoff manifests. Mission Control remains a client.
acceptance_criteria: The epic's mesh, remote-session/artifact protocol, and handoff child issues are each implemented and verified in dependency order; Mission Control can consume the resulting arcmux interfaces without owning remote transport.

title: Implement safe cross-device agent handoff
description: Make it easy to pause an agent conversation on one device and resume it on another using the mesh plus already-synced history, branches, and stored artifacts. Handoff must be explicit, inspectable, and safe when working trees or credentials are not transferable.
design: Build on the remote-session/artifact protocol, not terminal scraping. Use a declarative handoff manifest and two-phase prepare/accept acknowledgement. Never infer or silently copy credentials. Prefer synced history plus pushed branches; allow an explicit patch/bundle artifact as fallback.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The source produces a versioned handoff manifest containing trace/session lineage, latest user goal, conversation-history reference, repo/branch/head, worktree cleanliness, artifact references, validation state, and target device. 2. Transfer requires a pushed/fetchable branch or an explicit patch/bundle; dirty or inaccessible state blocks with a clear remedy. 3. The target validates repo/artifact availability, creates or reuses the correct worktree, starts a supervised agent with inherited trace context, and records source/target lineage. 4. Repeated handoff requests are idempotent and never spawn duplicate continuations. 5. Failed or offline targets leave the source session intact and a retryable request queued. 6. Tests cover clean branch, stored patch, missing history, dirty unexported state, offline target, duplicate delivery, and successful laptop-to-devbox/labs resume.
